title: Motion, Velocity and Equations of Motion
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Differentiate between distance, displacement, speed, and velocity.
Understand acceleration $a = F/m$ and Newton's second law.
Simulate physical block motion on inclined ramps with live vector arrows.

objectives.text: Apply force $F=35$ N on a $5$ kg block and calculate acceleration.
Observe vector arrows representing Gravity, Normal force, and Friction.

theory: First equation of motion: $v = u + at$. Second equation: $s = ut + \frac{1}{2}at^2$. Newton's Second Law: $F = ma$.
hints: Click "Play Motion" to start the WebGL physics simulation loop.
Adjust the Ramp Angle slider to observe gravity vector resolution.

tasks.instruction: Increase the Applied Force slider above 30 N.

observationPrompts: How does doubling the mass of an object change its acceleration under constant force?

realWorldExamples.title: Automobile Acceleration
realWorldExamples.description: Car engines apply forward traction force to overcome inertia and accelerate the vehicle mass.

commonMistakes: Confusing speed (scalar) with velocity (vector having both magnitude and direction).
